original_hypothesis.title: WebAuthn.verify may accept forged signatures on chains where address(0x100) is a non-precompile contract that returns non-empty bytes decoding to 1
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: WebAuthn.verify treats any successful staticcall to address(0x100) that returns non-empty bytes as 'valid' and then decodes it as uint256 and checks == 1. If on a given chain address(0x100) is not the RIP-7212 precompile but a deployed contract that always returns (uint256(1)), all WebAuthn signatures would be accepted without cryptographic verification.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a chain-environment hazard with catastrophic impact: passkey owner authentication would be bypassed. The fallback to FCL only happens when (success && valid) is false; a malicious/incorrect contract at 0x100 makes (success && valid) true.
deployment_assumptions: Target chain does NOT implement RIP-7212 precompile at 0x100
A contract can exist at 0x100 that returns crafted non-empty data (e.g., due to chain-specific system contracts or predeploys)
Wallet uses passkey owners (64-byte owner bytes) for authentication
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Project targets multiple chains; RIP-7212 availability varies. No chain-specific guard (e.g., codehash allowlist) is implemented for 0x100.

inventory_refs.function: verify(bytes memory challenge, bool requireUV, WebAuthnAuth memory webAuthnAuth, uint256 x, uint256 y)

proof_of_signal.lines: staticcall to VERIFIER=address(0x100); `bool valid = ret.length > 0; if (success && valid) return abi.decode(ret,(uint256)) == 1;`
proof_of_signal.pattern: trusts any non-empty return data from 0x100 without verifying code identity

preconditions: Chain has code at 0x100 that is not the intended precompile and returns attacker-controlled values
Wallet has at least one passkey owner and uses WebAuthn path
Attacker can submit any WebAuthnAuth struct; signature values need not be valid if 0x100 lies

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: assumes 0x100 is either correct precompile or empty; no codehash verification
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: false positives in signature verification => full wallet takeover for passkey owners
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: no chain allowlist; no extcodesize/extcodehash check; no dual-verify (precompile AND FCL) when ret.length>0

analysis_questions: On each target chain, what is deployed at 0x100 (precompile, empty, or system contract)?
If code exists at 0x100, does it conform to RIP-7212 ABI and return semantics?
Is it feasible for an attacker to deploy at 0x100 on any target chain (typically not on Ethereum, but some L2s may have predeploy space)?
validation_requirements: Chain-by-chain reconnaissance: extcodehash(0x100) comparison against expected precompile behavior
If any chain has non-precompile code at 0x100, simulate forged signature acceptance on that chain/fork
Consider modifying logic to require FCL verification unless a known codehash is present

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: On a chain where 0x100 is an always-true verifier contract, attacker submits a UserOp signed with arbitrary WebAuthnAuth. validateUserOp accepts it as valid, allowing attacker to execute transfers or add themselves as owner.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Complete compromise of wallets using passkeys on affected chain(s)
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All passkey-based accounts on impacted networks
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Requires emergency upgrade/migration; compromised funds likely unrecoverable

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No tests for behavior when 0x100 has unexpected code returning non-empty data

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: runtime chain check of 0x100 code identity

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
